replies.msg:
男拔榜眼湯同學咁愛国，緊係要留有用之軀貢獻祖國！
一人一信要求Duke University取消湯同學嘅入學資格。

(Template如下，拋磚引玉，如果錯grammar屌得細力啲）

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, one of your students-to-be.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that THE NATIONAL SECURITY LAW WILL IMPROVE THE SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT OF HONG KONG. The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong also claimed that THE RULE OF LAW HAS PRECEDENCE OVER FREEDOM, and ON THE BASIS OF THE “ONE COUNTRY TWO SYSTEM” PRINCIPLE , PROSPERITY AND STABILITY WILL ONLY BE ESTABLISHED WHEN THE COUNTRY’S SECURITY IS GUARANTEED.

It is my deepest concern that accepting a student who supports the suppression of basic human rights will possibly damage the reputation of your school. Meanwhile, I sincerely doubt that he may be involved in spying activity while studying in your school, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hope that the admission of Mr Tong could be reconsidered seriously.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

湯同學：國安法對社會氛圍有很大幫助

考獲 44 分、獲美國杜克大學錄取修讀物理的湯同學則表示，國安法是中央對香港的政治表態，「上年好多嘅違法行為係唔需要國安法，佢哋都係違法，中央係唔會縱容。」他指中央是次的表態非常清晰，相信對香港整個社會氛圍有很大的幫助，「法治係行先過自由，有法律保障、不侵犯他人嘅自由先係真正的自由，喺一國兩制的框架下，有一國嘅安定先有繁榮嘅穩定」。

湯同學咁支持国安法，點可以去勾結外國勢力㗎

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, one of your students-to-be.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that THE NATIONAL SECURITY LAW WILL IMPROVE THE SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT OF HONG KONG. The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong also claimed that THE RULE OF LAW HAS PRECEDENCE OVER FREEDOM, and ON THE BASIS OF THE “ONE COUNTRY TWO SYSTEM” PRINCIPLE , PROSPERITY AND STABILITY WILL ONLY BE ESTABLISHED WHEN THE COUNTRY’S SECURITY IS GUARANTEED.

It is my deepest concern that accepting a student who supports the suppression of basic human rights will possibly damage the reputation of your school. Meanwhile, I sincerely doubt that he may be involved in spying activity while studying in your school, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hope that the admission of Mr Tong could be reconsidered seriously.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, one of your students-to-be.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that THE NATIONAL SECURITY LAW WILL IMPROVE THE SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT OF HONG KONG. The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong also claimed that THE RULE OF LAW HAS PRECEDENCE OVER FREEDOM, and ON THE BASIS OF THE “ONE COUNTRY TWO SYSTEM” PRINCIPLE , PROSPERITY AND STABILITY WILL ONLY BE ESTABLISHED WHEN THE COUNTRY’S SECURITY IS GUARANTEED.

It is my deepest concern that accepting a student who supports the suppression of basic human rights will possibly damage the reputation of your school. Meanwhile, I sincerely doubt that he may be involved in spying activity while studying in your school, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hope that the admission of Mr Tong could be reconsidered seriously.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, which will soon start his tertiary education in the States.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School,HK who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that THE NATIONAL SECURITY LAW WILL IMPROVE THE SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT OF HONG KONG. The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong, the son of a committee member of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference also claimed that THE RULE BY LAW HAS PRECEDENCE OVER FREEDOM, and ON THE BASIS OF THE “ONE COUNTRY TWO SYSTEM” PRINCIPLE , PROSPERITY AND STABILITY WILL ONLY BE ESTABLISHED WHEN THE COUNTRY’S SECURITY IS GUARANTEED.

It is my deepest concern that approving the VISA application of a student who supports the suppression of basic human rights will possibly pose threat to your country. Meanwhile, I sincerely suspect that he may be involved in spying activity while studying in your school, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hope that the VISA application of Mr Tong could be reconsidered seriously. And if the application has been approved already, I hope the VISA can be revoked promptly.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

考獲 44 分、獲美國杜克大學錄取修讀物理的湯同學則表示，國安法是中央對香港的政治表態，「上年好多嘅違法行為係唔需要國安法，佢哋都係違法，中央係唔會縱容。」他指中央是次的表態非常清晰，相信對香港整個社會氛圍有很大的幫助，「法治係行先過自由，有法律保障、不侵犯他人嘅自由先係真正的自由，喺一國兩制的框架下，有一國嘅安定先有繁榮嘅穩定」。

湯同學咁支持国安法，點可以去勾結外國勢力㗎

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School,HK who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that THE NATIONAL SECURITY LAW WILL IMPROVE THE SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT OF HONG KONG. The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong, the son of a committee member of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference also claimed that THE RULE BY LAW HAS PRECEDENCE OVER FREEDOM, and ON THE BASIS OF THE “ONE COUNTRY TWO SYSTEM” PRINCIPLE , PROSPERITY AND STABILITY WILL ONLY BE ESTABLISHED WHEN THE COUNTRY’S SECURITY IS GUARANTEED.

It is my deepest concern that accepting a student who supports the suppression of basic human rights will possibly damage the reputation of your school. Meanwhile, I sincerely suspect that he may be involved in spying activity while studying in your school, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hoped that the admission of Mr Tong could be reconsidered seriously.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, one of your students-to-be.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that THE NATIONAL SECURITY LAW WILL IMPROVE THE SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT OF HONG KONG. The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong also claimed that THE RULE OF LAW HAS PRECEDENCE OVER FREEDOM, and ON THE BASIS OF THE “ONE COUNTRY TWO SYSTEM” PRINCIPLE , PROSPERITY AND STABILITY WILL ONLY BE ESTABLISHED WHEN THE COUNTRY’S SECURITY IS GUARANTEED.

It is my deepest concern that accepting a student who supports the suppression of basic human rights will possibly damage the reputation of your school. Meanwhile, I sincerely doubt that he may be involved in spying activity while studying in your school, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hope that the admission of Mr Tong could be reconsidered seriously.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, which will soon start his tertiary education in the States.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School,HK who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that “THE NATIONAL SECURITY LAW WILL IMPROVE THE SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT OF HONG KONG. “The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong, the son of a committee member of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference also claimed that “THE RULE BY LAW HAS PRECEDENCE OVER FREEDOM, and ON THE BASIS OF THE “ONE COUNTRY TWO SYSTEM” PRINCIPLE , PROSPERITY AND STABILITY WILL ONLY BE ESTABLISHED WHEN THE COUNTRY’S SECURITY IS GUARANTEED”.

It is my deepest concern that approving the VISA application of a student who supports the suppression of basic human rights will possibly pose threat to your country. Meanwhile, I sincerely suspect that he may be involved in spying activity while studying in duke university, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hoped that the VISA application of Mr Tong could be reconsidered seriously. And if the application has been approved already, I hope the VISA can be revoked promptly.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

Request for Termination of Visa Application of Tong Tak On

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, which will soon start his tertiary education in the States.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School,HK who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that “The National Security Law will improt the social environment of Hong Kong. “The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong, the son of a committee member of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference also claimed that "The rule by law has precedence over freedom, and on the basis of the "One Country, Two System" principle, prosperity and stability will only be established when the country's security is guaranteed".

Academic competency is crucial, but one’s integrity shall be significantly taken into account as well. We have grave concern that Tong, a student who alligns with the CCP and supports the suppression of basic human rights, will possibly tarnish your school’s well-established reputation, as well as sabotaging the welcoming and free learning environment of the Duke University. In addition to our conceivable worries, it is heavily suspected that Tong may be involved in those despicable spying activities that the CCP has long been infamous for, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

FPUHK@state.gov

（美領版）
Request for Termination of Visa Application of Tong Tak On

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, which will soon start his tertiary education in the States.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School,HK who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that “The National Security Law will improt the social environment of Hong Kong. “The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong, the son of a committee member of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference also claimed that "The rule by law has precedence over freedom, and on the basis of the "One Country, Two System" principle, prosperity and stability will only be established when the country's security is guaranteed".

Academic competency is crucial, but one’s integrity shall be significantly taken into account as well. In addition to our conceivable worries, it is heavily suspected that Tong may be involved in those despicable spying activities that the CCP has long been infamous for, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hoped that the VISA application of Tong could be terminated.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

（美領版）


Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, which will soon start his tertiary education in the States.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School,HK who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that THE NATIONAL SECURITY LAW WILL IMPROVE THE SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT OF HONG KONG. The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong, the son of a committee member of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference also claimed that THE RULE BY LAW HAS PRECEDENCE OVER FREEDOM, and ON THE BASIS OF THE “ONE COUNTRY TWO SYSTEM” PRINCIPLE , PROSPERITY AND STABILITY WILL ONLY BE ESTABLISHED WHEN THE COUNTRY’S SECURITY IS GUARANTEED.

It is my deepest concern that approving the VISA application of a student who supports the suppression of basic human rights will possibly pose threat to your country. Meanwhile, I sincerely suspect that he may be involved in spying activity while studying in your school, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hope that the VISA application of Mr Tong could be reconsidered seriously. And if the application has been approved already, I hope the VISA can be revoked promptly.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

Request for Termination of Visa Application of Tong Tak On

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, which will soon start his tertiary education in the States.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School,HK who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that “The National Security Law will improt the social environment of Hong Kong. “The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong, the son of a committee member of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference also claimed that "The rule by law has precedence over freedom, and on the basis of the "One Country, Two System" principle, prosperity and stability will only be established when the country's security is guaranteed".

Academic competency is crucial, but one’s integrity shall be significantly taken into account as well. In addition to our conceivable worries, it is heavily suspected that Tong may be involved in those despicable spying activities that the CCP has long been infamous for, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hoped that the VISA application of Tong could be terminated.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

（美領版）
Request for Termination of Visa Application of Tong Tak On

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concern about the integrity of Tong Tak On from Diocesan Boys’ School, which will soon start his tertiary education in the States.

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School,HK who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that “The National Security Law will improt the social environment of Hong Kong. “The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong, the son of a committee member of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference also claimed that "The rule by law has precedence over freedom, and on the basis of the "One Country, Two System" principle, prosperity and stability will only be established when the country's security is guaranteed".

Academic competency is crucial, but one’s integrity shall be significantly taken into account as well. In addition to our conceivable worries, it is heavily suspected that Tong may be involved in those despicable spying activities that the CCP has long been infamous for, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hoped that the VISA application of Tong could be terminated.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

Request for Termination of Visa Application of Tong Tak On

Dear Mr/Ms xxxx, (寫比有關人士)

I write to express my rightful concern about certain staff of the faculty of Duke University who tried to shield Tong Tak On, a USA student visa applicant who allegedly committed a criminalized offence in Hong Kong while at the same time publicly supported the criminalization of a controversial “national security” law in which local lawmakers have no voice, or representation.

I am particularly concerned about the fairness of the system overall. Mr. Tong Tak On managed to obviate the indictment which has thrown a great number of young people who, not as privileged as Mr. Tong Tak On, in jail - on the same criminal act Mr. Tong Tak On committed. It also invites suspicions whether certain faculty staff in Duke University were benefited from nepotism. Mr. Tong Tak On’s father is a bigwig in the Chinese Communist Party and has been making unflagging effort to force upon the indoctrination of Chinese Communist Party’s values.
May I please suggest that the country be alarmed of potential threats of technology theft from the Chinese Communist Party undercover. With that in mind, it merits intervention from the homeland security.

In sum, the integrity of the system, certain university staff and Mr. Tong Tak On himself should be subject to scrutiny.

Enclosed please find the reference of this claim.

I count on your goodwill.


Yours faithfully,
XXX

Request for Termination of Visa Application of Tong Tak On

Dear Mr/Ms xxxx, (寫比有關人士)

I write to express my rightful concern about certain staff of the faculty of Duke University who tried to shield Tong Tak On, a USA student visa applicant who allegedly committed a criminalized offence in Hong Kong while at the same time publicly supported the criminalization of a controversial “national security” law in which local lawmakers have no voice, or representation.

I am particularly concerned about the fairness of the system overall. Mr. Tong Tak On managed to obviate the indictment which has thrown a great number of young people who, not as privileged as Mr. Tong Tak On, in jail - on the same criminal act Mr. Tong Tak On committed. It also invites suspicions whether certain faculty staff in Duke University were benefited from nepotism. Mr. Tong Tak On’s father is a bigwig in the Chinese Communist Party and has been making unflagging effort to force upon the indoctrination of Chinese Communist Party’s values.
May I please suggest that the country be alarmed of potential threats of technology theft from the Chinese Communist Party undercover. With that in mind, it merits intervention from the homeland security.

In sum, the integrity of the system, certain university staff and Mr. Tong Tak On himself should be subject to scrutiny.

Enclosed please find the reference of this claim.

I count on your goodwill.


Yours faithfully,
XXX

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

所以，成功機會唔大。

Request for Termination of Visa Application of Tong Tak On

Dear Mr/Ms xxxx, (寫比有關人士)

I write to express my rightful concern about certain staff of the faculty of Duke University who tried to shield Tong Tak On, a USA student visa applicant who allegedly committed a criminalized offence in Hong Kong while at the same time publicly supported the criminalization of a controversial “national security” law in which local lawmakers have no voice, or representation.

I am particularly concerned about the fairness of the system overall. Mr. Tong Tak On managed to obviate the indictment which has thrown a great number of young people who, not as privileged as Mr. Tong Tak On, in jail - on the same criminal act Mr. Tong Tak On committed. It also invites suspicions whether certain faculty staff in Duke University were benefited from nepotism. Mr. Tong Tak On’s father is a bigwig in the Chinese Communist Party and has been making unflagging effort to force upon the indoctrination of Chinese Communist Party’s values.
May I please suggest that the country be alarmed of potential threats of technology theft from the Chinese Communist Party undercover. With that in mind, it merits intervention from the homeland security.

In sum, the integrity of the system, certain university staff and Mr. Tong Tak On himself should be subject to scrutiny.

Enclosed please find the reference of this claim.

I count on your goodwill.


Yours faithfully,
XXX

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that THE NATIONAL SECURITY LAW WILL IMPROVE THE SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT OF HONG KONG. The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong also claimed that THE RULE OF LAW HAS PRECEDENCE OVER FREEDOM, and ON THE BASIS OF THE “ONE COUNTRY TWO SYSTEM” PRINCIPLE , PROSPERITY AND STABILITY WILL ONLY BE ESTABLISHED WHEN THE COUNTRY’S SECURITY IS GUARANTEED.

It is my deepest concern that accepting a student who supports the suppression of basic human rights will possibly damage the reputation of your school. Meanwhile, I sincerely doubt that he may be involved in spying activity while studying in your school, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hope that the admission of Mr Tong could be reconsidered seriously.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,
XXX

According to a recent article featuring students with outstanding performance in the International Baccalaureate by Stand News (A Hong Kong-based news platform), Mr. Tong Tak On, a student from Diocesan Boys' School who scores a total of 44 marks and be accepted by the Duke University claimed that THE NATIONAL SECURITY LAW WILL IMPROVE THE SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT OF HONG KONG. The National Security Law, is an unjust law that deprives Hong Kongers of their basic rights and freedoms which has been condemned by the head of different states.

Tong also claimed that THE RULE OF LAW HAS PRECEDENCE OVER FREEDOM, and ON THE BASIS OF THE “ONE COUNTRY TWO SYSTEM” PRINCIPLE , PROSPERITY AND STABILITY WILL ONLY BE ESTABLISHED WHEN THE COUNTRY’S SECURITY IS GUARANTEED.

It is my deepest concern that accepting a student who supports the suppression of basic human rights will possibly damage the reputation of your school. Meanwhile, I sincerely doubt that he may be involved in spying activity while studying in your school, as his father is one of the committees of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. Therefore, it is hope that the admission of Mr Tong could be reconsidered seriously.

Enclosed please find the reference of his claim.

Thank you.

Yours faithfully,

Request for Termination of Visa Application of Tong Tak On

Dear Mr/Ms xxxx, (寫比有關人士)

I write to express my rightful concern about certain staff of the faculty of Duke University who tried to shield Tong Tak On, a USA student visa applicant who allegedly committed a criminalized offence in Hong Kong while at the same time publicly supported the criminalization of a controversial “national security” law in which local lawmakers have no voice, or representation.

I am particularly concerned about the fairness of the system overall. Mr. Tong Tak On managed to obviate the indictment which has thrown a great number of young people who, not as privileged as Mr. Tong Tak On, in jail - on the same criminal act Mr. Tong Tak On committed. It also invites suspicions whether certain faculty staff in Duke University were benefited from nepotism. Mr. Tong Tak On’s father is a bigwig in the Chinese Communist Party and has been making unflagging effort to force upon the indoctrination of Chinese Communist Party’s values.
May I please suggest that the country be alarmed of potential threats of technology theft from the Chinese Communist Party undercover. With that in mind, it merits intervention from the homeland security.

In sum, the integrity of the system, certain university staff and Mr. Tong Tak On himself should be subject to scrutiny.

Enclosed please find the reference of this claim.

I count on your goodwill.


Yours faithfully,
XXX

I write to express my rightful concern about certain staff of the faculty of Duke University who tried to shield Tong Tak On, a USA student visa applicant who allegedly committed a criminalized offence in Hong Kong while at the same time publicly supported the criminalization of a controversial “national security” law in which local lawmakers have no voice, or representation.

I am particularly concerned about the fairness of the system overall. Mr. Tong Tak On managed to obviate the indictment which has thrown a great number of young people who, not as privileged as Mr. Tong Tak On, in jail - on the same criminal act Mr. Tong Tak On committed. It also invites suspicions whether certain faculty staff in Duke University were benefited from nepotism. Mr. Tong Tak On’s father is a bigwig in the Chinese Communist Party and has been making unflagging effort to force upon the indoctrination of Chinese Communist Party’s values.
May I please suggest that the country be alarmed of potential threats of technology theft from the Chinese Communist Party undercover. With that in mind, it merits intervention from the homeland security.

In sum, the integrity of the system, certain university staff and Mr. Tong Tak On himself should be subject to scrutiny.

Enclosed please find the reference of this claim.

I count on your goodwill.


Yours faithfully,
XXX
